verses.text: 'An end is come' upon the land's four corners; God will judge her ways and 'recompense... thine abominations.' The reckoning arrives. Long-delayed judgment falls all at once when its hour comes.
'An end is come' upon the land's four corners; God will judge her ways and 'recompense... thine abominations.' The reckoning arrives. Long-delayed judgment falls all at once when its hour comes.
'An end is come' upon the land's four corners; God will judge her ways and 'recompense... thine abominations.' The reckoning arrives. Long-delayed judgment falls all at once when its hour comes.
'An end is come' upon the land's four corners; God will judge her ways and 'recompense... thine abominations.' The reckoning arrives. Long-delayed judgment falls all at once when its hour comes.
'An evil, an only evil,' draws near; the day of trouble is at hand, and God will repay according to their ways. No more reprieve. The time of patience has run out.
'An evil, an only evil,' draws near; the day of trouble is at hand, and God will repay according to their ways. No more reprieve. The time of patience has run out.

'An evil, an only evil,' draws near; the day of trouble is at hand, and God will repay according to their ways. No more reprieve. The time of patience has run out.

The day has come; pride has budded and the rod blossomed; let neither buyer rejoice nor seller mourn, for wrath is on all. Earthly gain is meaningless. When judgment comes, all worldly business is swept away.

The trumpet sounds, but none will fight; those who escape 'shall be... mourning every one for his iniquity.' Courage drains away. Guilt unmans a people in the face of the foe.

Every hand grows feeble; they fling their silver in the streets, for 'it cannot deliver them in the day of the wrath of the LORD.' Wealth fails utterly. Riches are worthless when God arises to judge.
Every hand grows feeble; they fling their silver in the streets, for 'it cannot deliver them in the day of the wrath of the LORD.' Wealth fails utterly. Riches are worthless when God arises to judge.
Every hand grows feeble; they fling their silver in the streets, for 'it cannot deliver them in the day of the wrath of the LORD.' Wealth fails utterly. Riches are worthless when God arises to judge.
They turned their ornaments into idols; so God will give it as spoil, and robbers shall 'defile my secret place.' What was misused is lost. The blessings men idolize become their plunder.

They turned their ornaments into idols; so God will give it as spoil, and robbers shall 'defile my secret place.' What was misused is lost. The blessings men idolize become their plunder.
The land is full of bloody crimes; God will bring 'the worst of the heathen,' and they shall seek peace and find none. Violence repaid. A society built on bloodshed reaps invasion and dread.

Mischief comes upon mischief; they shall seek a vision from the prophet in vain, for God will deal with them after their own way. Guidance withdrawn. When men spurn God's word, they lose it when they need it most.
